description:
You’re an MLOps engineer who has been handed a small collection of experiment artifacts that live inside:

/home/user/experiments

Inside that directory you will already find three sub-directories (created for you beforehand):

1. /home/user/experiments/exp_alpha  
2. /home/user/experiments/exp_beta  
3. /home/user/experiments/exp_gamma  

Each experiment folder contains exactly two files:

A. metrics.csv – a comma-separated file whose first row is a header line `epoch,accuracy,loss`.  
   • The file always has at least two data rows (epoch 1 … n).  
   • Accuracy and loss are recorded as floating-point numbers.

B. config.yaml – a small YAML file that always has, somewhere on its own line, the key `learning_rate:` followed by a single space and a floating-point value.

Your goal is to create one consolidated, single-line log entry for **each** experiment (so exactly three lines total) that extracts:

• The experiment’s directory name (exp_alpha, …)  
• The accuracy value from the LAST data row of metrics.csv  
• The loss value  from the LAST data row of metrics.csv  
• The learning_rate value from config.yaml

You must gather that information with standard Unix text-processing tools, specifically awk and/or sed (no Python, jq, etc.). Place the three log entries in a brand-new file:

/home/user/experiments/summary/experiment_overview.log

The summary directory does not yet exist, so you need to create it.

Exact required output format (each experiment is one line, and there are NO extra blank lines):

<experiment_name>,accuracy=<FINAL_ACCURACY>,loss=<FINAL_LOSS>,lr=<LEARNING_RATE>

• Values must appear in that order, separated only by literal commas (`,`)—no extra spaces.  
• Floating-point numbers must be written *exactly* as they appear in the source files (do not re-format them, don’t round them, don’t add trailing zeros).  
• The three lines must be sorted alphabetically by experiment name (exp_alpha first, then exp_beta, then exp_gamma).  
• A terminating newline after the last line is optional.

Example of the desired pattern (values here are merely illustrative):

exp_alpha,accuracy=0.9234,loss=0.1123,lr=0.001  
exp_beta,accuracy=0.9111,loss=0.1278,lr=0.0005  
exp_gamma,accuracy=0.9570,loss=0.0931,lr=0.002  

When you are done, anyone should be able to run:

  cat /home/user/experiments/summary/experiment_overview.log

and see three lines that match the format above, reflecting the *actual* values in the artifact files. The automated test will compare the content of experiment_overview.log byte-for-byte (UTF-8, Unix newlines) with the expected golden file, so formatting must be exact.

Hints (do NOT show exact commands):
• Consider “tail -n 1” to grab the final row from metrics.csv, or use AWK’s “END” block.  
• You can use a single temporary file per experiment, but the only file the test will grade is experiment_overview.log.  
• The task is small enough that a tight pipeline of 5-10 shell commands will finish it.

End state that will be verified:
1. Directory /home/user/experiments/summary/ exists.  
2. File /home/user/experiments/summary/experiment_overview.log exists.  
3. The file contains exactly three non-blank lines, in alphabetical order, each following the exact format specified above and accurately reflecting the source data.
truth:
Initial file tree and contents (already present before the agent starts):

/home/user/experiments/exp_alpha/metrics.csv
--------------------------------------------
epoch,accuracy,loss
1,0.8071,0.6722
2,0.8983,0.3399
3,0.9234,0.1123

/home/user/experiments/exp_alpha/config.yaml
--------------------------------------------
model: resnet18
learning_rate: 0.001
epochs: 3

/home/user/experiments/exp_beta/metrics.csv
-------------------------------------------
epoch,accuracy,loss
1,0.7550,0.7109
2,0.8662,0.4012
3,0.9111,0.1278

/home/user/experiments/exp_beta/config.yaml
-------------------------------------------
model: mobilenetv2
optimizer: adam
learning_rate: 0.0005
epochs: 3

/home/user/experiments/exp_gamma/metrics.csv
--------------------------------------------
epoch,accuracy,loss
1,0.8744,0.5080
2,0.9327,0.2414
3,0.9570,0.0931

/home/user/experiments/exp_gamma/config.yaml
--------------------------------------------
model: efficientnet_b0
epochs: 3
learning_rate: 0.002

The expected final file the tests will look for:

/home/user/experiments/summary/experiment_overview.log
------------------------------------------------------
exp_alpha,accuracy=0.9234,loss=0.1123,lr=0.001
exp_beta,accuracy=0.9111,loss=0.1278,lr=0.0005
exp_gamma,accuracy=0.9570,loss=0.0931,lr=0.002

(Unix newline after the last line is accepted but not required.)

No other new files or directories will be checked.
